summary: ACCEPT for fixed implementation commit f69882f5387693f1aa10eca1d5adfdca7b206214. The diff is confined to allowed UI/web/client-core test and process-message paths, with no forbidden package, protocol, save, content, platform, gate, roadmap, release, telemetry, server, multiplayer, account, cloud-save, branding, final-art, or manual-node-battle scope. The appointment player flow consumes existing M3 appointment read models, reason/result data, administrative preview fields, and client-core command builders; it does not introduce UI-owned authoritative WorldState or duplicated appointment/governance formulas. Player confirmation still submits the standard sim.appoint-office GameCommand surface through createM3AppointmentCommand. Bulk submit remains reachable only inside the explicit developer overlay, while default player mode renders bulk preview as informational context. Raw reason-code DOM attributes were removed from shared reason chips and raw appointment reason codes are only exposed through the debug-only legacy workspace details affordance.

Current checkpoint HEAD is 5ec623490a30b8231956c77eaf3653ba8e86d8d3; review target remains implementation commit f69882f5387693f1aa10eca1d5adfdca7b206214.

tests_run.command: Targeted git diff/git grep inspections for raw reason leakage, WorldState writes, command builders, bulk submit/debug-only separation, and forbidden scope
tests_run.result: No new sim-core/protocol/save/platform/content changes, WorldState writes, telemetry/server/multiplayer/cloud/account/release/branding/manual-node-battle scope, or default player-mode data-reason-code leakage found. Player confirm path calls createM3AppointmentCommand; bulk submit is passed only to M3AppointmentWorkspace under debugMode.
format:check, lint, typecheck, and architecture:check passed. Architecture boundaries passed; self-test fixture produced 15 expected violations.

decisions: Accepted without ADR because the implementation does not change package boundaries, public protocol/schema/save formats, simulation authority, deterministic behavior, platform commitments, or frozen product decisions.
Diff-scope decision: cecc0246..f69882f5 stays inside allowed_paths, including process-message/state files, and does not touch any listed forbidden_paths.

Architecture decision: the player appointment flow reads ClientM3AppointmentReadModelSnapshot data and submits ClientM3SubmittedCommand/GameCommand through existing client-core builders; React state tracks UI flow stage and selection only, not authoritative domain state.

Formula boundary decision: displayed eligibility, rejection, administrative load, efficiency, reliability, readiness, result, and reason information comes from existing read-model fields. The only synthetic status/reason changes are apps/web Storybook/E2E fixture variants for empty/rejected/extreme states.

Command parity decision: player confirm uses createM3AppointmentCommand with kind sim.appoint-office, actor/day/revision from the read model, and no hidden player-only command path. Bulk command builder remains existing behavior and is not part of the default player flow.

Debug separation decision: M3AppointmentWorkspace, raw appointment reason-code details, state hash, fixture kind, and bulk submit button are reachable only when debugMode renders the developer overlay; default player mode uses localized reason text and no data-reason-code attributes.

Coverage decision: Storybook adds normal, empty, rejected, extreme, and narrow appointment states; Playwright covers English default flow, Chinese rejected flow, debug-hidden workspace, bulk-preview informational behavior, and no-overlap viewport smoke.
risks: The current apps/web shell still records local UI status after command submission instead of submitting to the real simulation worker. This is pre-existing shell-fixture behavior and remains acceptable only while runtime integration continues to preserve the same GameCommand boundary.

apps/web appointment-empty/rejected/extreme fixtures intentionally rewrite read-model snapshots for Storybook and E2E coverage. Keep those helpers confined to test/display fixture input; do not reuse them as domain eligibility or governance logic.
The explicit developer overlay still contains legacy technical labels, raw IDs, and raw reason details by design. QA should evaluate default player mode separately from the debug affordance.
requested_action: Perform independent QA review of M7-APPOINTMENT-FLOW-QUALITY-001 at implementation commit f69882f5387693f1aa10eca1d5adfdca7b206214, focusing on user-visible behavior, localization/debug separation, regression coverage, and the recorded systems architecture evidence.
